rows.notes: Page title/header centered at top. Handwriting of heading is legible; no monetary figures appear on the same line.

Description 'E Treasurer' is legible. Pounds (251) and shillings (6) align clearly in the first two money columns. A '6' is visible in the narrow pence column; there is a faint small annotation further at extreme margin but it is separate from the pence column and ignored.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt from the Treasurer, amounting to £251 6s 6d.

rows.notes: Description partially abbreviated but readable as Long-Wittenham ad Annum. Pounds (24) and shillings (3) are clearly placed. Pence reads as '11' in the pence column; minor ink smearing makes the second '1' slightly lighter but alignment into the pence column supports '11'.
rows.english_description: Annual rent received from the Long Wittenham estate.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent received from the Long Wittenham estate, amounting to £24 3s 11d.

rows.notes: Description interpreted as 'Addington Vicar' though the surname/word after Addington is slightly unclear. The pounds column shows '4' clearly. Shillings/pence appear to be zeros; there is a very small faint mark at the extreme right margin (not in the pence column) which I did not interpret as a fraction or extra pence.
rows.english_description: Payment from the vicar of Addington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from the vicar of Addington, amounting to £4 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Name 'Lucas Eaton' is clear. Pounds '2' is clearly inside the pounds column; shillings and pence columns contain zeros/blank and read as 0 and 0 with good alignment.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt from Lucas Eaton, amounting to £2 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Surname 'Bannister' clear. Pounds column blank/zero; shillings '15' is clearly aligned between the second and third vertical lines. Pence is blank/zero.

Name is partially unclear (transcribed here as 'Pentrinhul Napper ad Annum' to reflect the ink); pounds and shillings read as '1' and '16' respectively and align with columns. Pence column is blank. Because the description is uncertain and the first word may be mis-transcribed, confidence lowered.
rows.english_description: Annual payment from Napper for the Pentrinhul holding.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payment from Napper for the Pentrinhul holding, amounting to £1 16s 0d.

rows.notes: Description 'Uddlington Augm:' is a best reading of a somewhat stylised place/term. Numerical columns show 62 pounds and 6 shillings clearly; pence blank. Some ink variation in the hundreds digit, but alignment supports 62.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Augmentation (ecclesiastical top-up) from Uddlington, amounting to £62 6s 0d.

rows.notes: Description with three names is legible. Pound (8), shillings (16) and pence (8) are all present and well aligned in their columns. Ink is consistent and values are within valid ranges.
rows.english_description: Annual rents or payments from Sad-lane, Sparkes and Merton.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rents or payments from Sad-lane, Sparkes and Merton, amounting to £8 16s 8d.

rows.description: Rev Mr Horner a Benefaction of his towards new painting the Chappel
rows.notes: Description read as a benefaction from 'Rev Mr Horner' toward the chapel painting; wording is slightly expanded for clarity but mirrors the original. Amount 45 pounds exactly fills the pounds column; shillings and pence are blank/zero.
rows.english_description: Benefaction from Rev. Mr. Horner toward repainting the chapel.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Benefaction from Rev. Mr. Horner toward repainting the chapel, amounting to £45 0s 0d.

rows.description: Recd in part Mr Cooperat Incremnt for Mids: 1731
rows.notes: Description is partly abbreviated; read as 'Recd in part Mr Cooperat Incremnt for Mids: 1731'. Numbers in the three money columns read as 41, 9, 9 but the handwriting in the description and the tail of the pence digit are slightly indistinct, so moderate confidence.
rows.english_description: Part payment received from Mr. Cooperat as an increment for Midsummer 1731.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Part payment received from Mr. Cooperat as an increment for Midsummer 1731, amounting to £41 9s 9d.

rows.description: Mr Wrosetions Incremts for Mid: 1728 & Mid: 1729
rows.notes: Description is difficult to parse (transcribed as 'Mr Wrosetions Incremts'); numerical alignment shows '115' in pounds, '5' in shillings and '4' pence. Slight ambiguity in the reading of the name and the placement of the hundreds digit causes reduced confidence.
rows.english_description: Increments received from Mr. Wrosetions covering Midsummer 1728 and Midsummer 1729.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Increments received from Mr. Wrosetions covering Midsummer 1728 and Midsummer 1729, amounting to £115 5s 4d.

rows.description: for Hancocks Battes formerly all to one off ???
rows.notes: Description is poorly legible; appears to refer to 'Hancocks Battles' or similar. Money columns read 2 pounds, 18 shillings, 6 pence; these numbers align with the three columns but the description is very uncertain so confidence lowered.
rows.english_description: Receipt for Hancock's battels (college charges) previously combined into one account.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt for Hancock's battels (college charges) previously combined into one account, amounting to £2 18s 6d.

rows.notes: Description reads as 'Old Pits Battles at Xmas 1722' (some words archaic/abbreviated). Amounts show blank/zero in pounds, '11' shillings clear, and pence blank/zero. Columns are well aligned.
rows.english_description: Battels (college charges) from Old Pits for Christmas 1722.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Battels (college charges) from Old Pits for Christmas 1722, amounting to £0 11s 0d.

rows.notes: Name reads as 'Mr Bayleys' and an increment date (mids 1725). Monetary columns show 59, 11, 9 clearly. Slight curl on the final pence digit but alignment and context make reading confident.
rows.english_description: Increment received from Mr. Bayley relating to Midsummer 1725.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Increment received from Mr. Bayley relating to Midsummer 1725, amounting to £59 11s 9d.

rows.notes: Description is abbreviated and proper name uncertain ('Kehewitch' is best-effort). Amounts show 8 pounds and zeros for shillings/pence; description ambiguity reduces confidence.
rows.english_description: Payment from the gentleman commoner Kehewitch.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from the gentleman commoner Kehewitch, amounting to £8 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Description mostly legible as 'Commoners Library at Midst 1701' though the date/word is slightly abbreviated. Monetary columns read 6 pounds, 5 shillings, pence blank. Alignment is clear.
rows.english_description: Amount received for the commoners' library fee at Midsummer 1701.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Amount received for the commoners' library fee at Midsummer 1701, amounting to £6 5s 0d.

rows.description: all the other sums remaining due from Xmas 1723 to Xmas 1730
rows.notes: This explanatory line is fully legible and reads as a summary of outstanding sums. Monetary values read as 20 pounds, 7 shillings, 6 pence; columns clear and values within valid ranges.
rows.english_description: Various remaining sums due from Christmas 1723 through Christmas 1730.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Various remaining sums due from Christmas 1723 through Christmas 1730, amounting to £20 7s 6d.

rows.description: Recd from Mr William Talbot a Benefit towards building a new Library
rows.notes: Readable donation entry from 'Mr William Talbot'. Pounds 21 is clear in the pounds column; shillings and pence are zero. Description and numbers are well aligned and legible.
rows.english_description: Donation from Mr. William Talbot toward building a new library.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Donation from Mr. William Talbot toward building a new library, amounting to £21 0s 0d.

rows.description: Recd by order of Mr Charles Talbot Arb[?] towards building a library
rows.notes: Description 'Recd by order of Mr Charles Talbot' followed by a short abbreviation is legible. Monetary columns show 52 pounds and 10 shillings; pence blank. Slight uncertainty over the final abbreviated word after 'Talbot' but numbers are clear.
rows.english_description: Receipt by order of Mr. Charles Talbot toward building a library.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt by order of Mr. Charles Talbot toward building a library, amounting to £52 10s 0d.
